value.Title: Revolve to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 2026

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-GMR3-SFP4-10RH-00000-00Revolve to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 20262026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisRevolve to Present at the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational on May 18, 2026

VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / May 11, 2026 / Revolve Renewable Power Corp. (CSE:REVV)(OTCQB:REVVF) ("Revolve" or the "Company"), a North American owner, operator and developer of renewable energy projects, is pleased to announce that it will be participating in the 16th Annual LD Micro Invitational at the Luxe Sunset Boulevard Hotel in Los Angeles, CA, May 18th and 19th, 2026.

Revolve CEO Myke Clark is scheduled to present on Monday, May 18, 2026, at 11:00 AM PT, where he will provide an overview of the Company's recent developments and outlook for the remainder of 2026.

To access the live presentation, please use the following information:

LD Micro Invitational XVI
Date: Monday, May 18, 2026
Time: 11:00 AM PT
Webcast: https://ldmicroevents.com/

If you would like more information about the event or to book 1x1 investor meetings with Revolve CEO Myke Clark, please contact LD Micro at registration@ldmicro.com. For more information on LD Micro, visit www.ldmicro.com.

About LD Micro

LD Micro, a wholly owned subsidiary of Freedom US Markets, was founded in 2006 with the sole purpose of being an independent resource in the micro-cap space. Through its dynamic, investor-driven conferences and curated company exposure, LD has served as an invaluable asset to all those interested in discovering the next generation of great companies. For more information on LD Micro, visit www.ldmicro.com.

For further information contact:

Myke Clark, CEO
IR@revolve-renewablepower.com
778-372-8499

About Revolve

Revolve was formed in 2012 to capitalize on the growing global demand for renewable power. Revolve develops utility-scale wind, solar, hydro and battery storage projects in the US, Canada and Mexico. Revolve also installs and operates sub 20 megawatt ("MW") "behind the meter" distributed generation (or "DG") assets. Revolve's portfolio includes the following:

Operating Assets: 13 MW (net) of operating assets under long term power purchase agreements across Canada and Mexico covering wind, solar, battery storage and hydro generation;

Development: a diverse portfolio of utility scale development projects across the US, Canada and Mexico with a combined capacity of over 3,000MWs as well as a 140MW+ distributed generation portfolio that is under development.

Revolve has an accomplished management team with a demonstrated track record of taking projects from "greenfield" through to "ready to build" status and successfully concluding project sales to large operators of utility-scale renewable energy projects. To-date, Revolve has developed and sold over 1,550MW of projects.

Forward Looking Information

The forward-looking statements contained in this news release constitute ‘‘forward-looking information'' within the meaning of applicable securities laws in each of the provinces and territories of Canada and the respective policies, regulations and rules under such laws and ‘‘forward-looking statements'' within the meaning of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 (collectively, ‘‘forward-looking statements"). The words "will", "expects", "estimates", "projections", "forecast", "intends", "anticipates", "believes", "targets" (and grammatical variations of such terms) and similar expressions are often intended to identify forward-looking statements, although not all forward- looking statements contain these identifying words. Forward looking statements in this press release include statements with respect to the Company's business objectives and project development goals, including the construction and operation of the distributed generation Portfolio.

This forward-looking information and other forward-looking information are based on our opinions, estimates and assumptions considering our experience and perception of historical trends, current conditions and expected future developments, as well as other factors that we currently believe are appropriate and reasonable in the circumstances. Despite a careful process to prepare and review the forward-looking information, there can be no assurance that the underlying opinions, estimates and assumptions will prove to be correct. Material factors underlying forward-looking information and management's expectations include: the receipt of applicable regulatory approvals; the absence of material adverse regulatory decisions being received and the expectation of regulatory stability; the absence of any material equipment breakdown or failure; availability of financing on commercially reasonable terms and the stability of credit ratings of the Company and its subsidiaries; the absence of unexpected material liabilities or uninsured losses; the continued availability of commodity supplies and stability of commodity prices; the absence of interest rate increases or significant currency exchange rate fluctuations; the absence of significant operational, financial or supply chain disruptions or liability, including relating to import controls and tariffs; the continued ability to maintain systems and facilities to ensure their continued performance; the absence of a severe and prolonged downturn in general economic, credit, social or market conditions; the successful and timely development and construction of new projects; the absence of capital project or financing cost overruns; sufficient liquidity and capital resources; the continuation of long term weather patterns and trends; the absence of significant counterparty defaults; the continued competitiveness of electricity pricing when compared with alternative sources of energy; the realization of the anticipated benefits of the Company's acquisitions and joint ventures; the absence of a change in applicable laws, political conditions, public policies and directions by governments, materially negatively affecting the Company; the ability to obtain and maintain licenses and permits; maintenance of adequate insurance coverage; the absence of material fluctuations in market energy prices; the absence of material disputes with taxation authorities or changes to applicable tax laws; continued maintenance of information technology infrastructure and the absence of a material breach of cybersecurity; the successful implementation of new information technology systems and infrastructure; favourable relations with external stakeholders; our ability to retain key personnel; our ability to maintain and expand distribution capabilities; and our ability to continue investing in infrastructure to support our growth.

Risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by forward-looking statements include, without limitation: risks relating to the Company's ability to develop and advance its renewable energy projects (including permitting, interconnection, construction, supply chain and cost inflation risks); risks relating to acquisitions (including the ability to identify, negotiate and complete acquisitions on acceptable terms); and general market, economic, interest rate, foreign exchange, and industry conditions. Additional risks and uncertainties are described in the Company's continuous disclosure filings available on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

There can be no assurance that such statements will prove to be accurate, and actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements. Readers are cautioned that given these risks, undue reliance should not be placed on these forward-looking statements, which apply only as of their dates. Other than as specifically required by law, the Company undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements to reflect new information, subsequent or otherwise. Such statements and information reflect the current view of the Company. By their nature, forward- looking statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors which may cause our actual results, performance or achievements, or other future events, to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievements expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. value.Title: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-7J23-RTTV-W25P-00000-00HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:29ZLexisNexisSTOCKHENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO

PermID4296577863Websitehttp://www.henselphelps.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

Hensel Phelps Construction Co., Inc., founded in 1937, is an employee-owned general contractor headquartered in Greeley, Colorado. With over 4,000 employees across 10 U.S. offices, it delivers construction and renovation projects in aviation, commercial, healthcare, education, and government sectors. Generating over $6 billion annually, notable projects include the Pentagon renovation and San Jose International Airport modernization. In 2024, Hensel Phelps advanced its use of virtual design and construction (VDC) technologies, with VDC Manager Mark Blacklin winning a drone challenge for high-quality imaging. Its culture emphasizes safety, collaboration, and innovation, fostering a commitment to excellence through employee ownership. Competing with Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps stands out for its integrated project delivery and sustainable practices. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy projects and enhancing safety programs like Construction Safety Week.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. The deployment at SFO showcases how Track3D can enhance progress tracking and coordination across trades without necessitating significant changes to existing workflows.On the product development front, Track3D has announced a March release aimed at improving enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. New features include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which allow for centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, enhancements such as capture-free progress tracking capabilities enable teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, marking a significant improvement in operational flexibility for enterprise users.Track3D is also advancing its go-to-market strategy by focusing on specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. This event is pivotal for drywall and ceiling professionals, and Track3D aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and enhance margin protection. Overall, the recent developments with Hensel Phelps, the SFO case study, and the March product upgrades indicate a period of meaningful commercial traction for Track3D, potentially solidifying its competitive position in the construction technology landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FREE AI TOOL FOR ENHANCED JOBSITE SAFETY

Turner Construction has launched SafeT Coach, a free AI-powered tool designed to enhance safety on construction job sites. This innovative solution is accessible from any mobile device and aims to facilitate real-time discussions centered around safety protocols. On 9th May 2026, the company highlighted that SafeT Coach answers safety-related questions using Turner’s Environmental Health & Safety standards, rather than generic sources available on the internet.The tool is engineered to foster a coaching mindset among supervisors and craft professionals, focusing on dialogue about safer work methods rather than mere enforcement of rules. Outputs generated by SafeT Coach are framed as potential hazards, supporting the professional judgment of users. The development of this tool involved collaboration with over 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots, ensuring its relevance and effectiveness.Since its pilot introduction, SafeT Coach has recorded more than 25,000 interactions involving Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. For instance, Darren Dreas, a superintendent working on a higher-education lab project, utilized the tool to evaluate whether a vertical shaft met the criteria for a permit-required confined space. With SafeT Coach, he was able to quickly access a decision flow chart, a start-of-day permit checklist, and relevant policy citations, facilitating an informed discussion with a trade partner’s safety manager.Dreas commented on the efficiency of the tool, stating, “Using SafeT Coach saved hours of research and analysis, helping us to quickly make informed decisions.” This positive feedback underscores the potential impact of SafeT Coach on improving safety protocols in the construction industry, enabling better decision-making on-site and promoting a culture of safety.As construction continues to evolve with technology integration, Turner Construction's introduction of SafeT Coach represents a significant step towards leveraging AI for practical safety solutions. The tool's free accessibility opens opportunities for various companies across the industry to enhance their safety measures without incurring additional costs, potentially leading to broader adoption of advanced safety practices.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR MAJOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected to serve as the general contractor for the ambitious Westcourt project, a $500 million mixed-use development located adjacent to the Kia Center in downtown Orlando. This announcement was made on 5th May 2026, marking a significant step forward for a project that has faced multiple delays in recent years.The Westcourt development is planned to cover approximately 8.5 acres and will feature a sports and entertainment district ranging from 900,000 to 1 million square feet. Key components of the project include a 265-key Kimpton-branded hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space. Additionally, the plan encompasses over 125,000 square feet dedicated to dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity venue for live events, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces.Turner Construction has indicated that during peak construction periods, the project will support around 800 workers on-site and is expected to create approximately 1,600 direct construction jobs throughout its duration. The firm anticipates engaging many trade partners from the Central Florida area, which could bolster local employment opportunities.In April 2024, the project received necessary land use entitlements, with the Kimpton hotel partnership announced later that same year, marking the brand's inaugural location in Orlando. Public support has also been secured, with Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency committing $2.5 million to the project, potentially reimbursing the developer up to $40 million over time through future property tax growth generated by Westcourt.Looking ahead, development documents suggest a targeted groundbreaking in the second quarter of 2026, with the projected opening set for late 2028. While construction cranes are not expected to rise immediately, the attachment of a reputable builder like Turner Construction brings renewed momentum to one of downtown Orlando's most anticipated projects.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INDIAN OPERATIONS WITH NEW WEWORK WORKSPACE

Turner Construction is solidifying its presence in India with the establishment of a new managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai. This expansion marks a significant milestone for the global construction firm as it partners with WeWork India. The new facility, which spans 46,000 square feet and accommodates over 650 professionals, was opened on the 7th of May, 2026, reflecting Turner’s long-term commitment to India's infrastructure and talent ecosystem.The decision by Turner Construction to consolidate its teams in a large-scale managed office underscores the growing recognition among enterprises that workspace design can be a strategic enabler of collaboration and operational agility. Exclusively designed for Turner, this office aligns with the company's global standards while leveraging WeWork India's design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, stated that the new workspace is more than just a physical location; it embodies the company's investment in an environment that fosters collaboration, creativity, and excellence.Turner’s relationship with WeWork India began in 2022 with a modest 16-seat private office, which has now evolved into a comprehensive enterprise-grade managed office solution. As Turner's operations in India expanded, the need for a flexible workspace that supports employee convenience and corporate identity became paramount. Airoli was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and burgeoning business ecosystem. The managed office incorporates Turner’s precision-focused approach along with WeWork India's design thinking, featuring collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The rise in demand for managed offices like the one established by Turner Construction reflects a broader shift in enterprise perspectives on workspaces. As noted by Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, companies are increasingly viewing their work environments not just as operational assets, but as critical drivers of organizational performance and culture. Turner’s transition from a small office to a larger, customized workspace exemplifies the trend towards flexible models that facilitate growth while maintaining a strong corporate identity.With this latest addition, WeWork India continues to expand its footprint among enterprise clients, with 76 percent of its member base in Mumbai representing various sectors, including technology and finance. Operating across eight cities with over 100,000 desks, WeWork India is positioning flexible workspaces as an essential component of modern business infrastructure in the country.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: May 05, 2026: Ken Sanocki Joins Wilson Sonsini as ECS Partner, Boosting Energy-Transition Financing

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-8CX3-RRYB-434F-00000-00May 05, 2026: Ken Sanocki Joins Wilson Sonsini as ECS Partner, Boosting Energy-Transition Financing2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 05, 2026: Ken Sanocki Joins Wilson Sonsini as ECS Partner, Boosting Energy-Transition Financing

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati named Ken Sanocki a partner in its Energy and Climate Solutions practice, expanding the firm's capacity to guide clients through complex energy-infrastructure financing during the energy transition.

Sanocki previously served as vice president of legal at Sunrun, the largest residential solar and storage provider in the United States, where he oversaw all project-finance matters, including tax equity financings and debt structures for large-scale distributed-energy projects. Since 5th May 2026, he has led more than 50 tax equity financings totaling over $10 billion across diverse distributed-energy portfolios.

Doug Clark, the firm's managing partner, commented, "Ken has operated at the center of the energy transition, helping shape the financing structures that have enabled distributed energy to scale into mainstream infrastructure." His expertise in tax equity and structured financings, paired with commercial instincts, will help clients navigate energy-financing complexities.

At Wilson Sonsini, Sanocki will advise companies, sponsors, and investors on structured and project finance across renewable energy, storage, and climate infrastructure, and support capital formation and strategic transactions, including asset-portfolio development and preparation for IPOs or M&A. The firm has been expanding its clean-energy advisory capabilities through partner additions who specialize in structured finance and regulatory matters.

Sanocki's background also includes serving as senior counsel for global structured and project finance at SunEdison and beginning his career as a project-finance associate at Milbank. He earned a J.D. from NYU School of Law and a B.A. from Boston College. His arrival, alongside other recent partner hires, signals Wilson Sonsini's strengthened position in sophisticated energy-transition finance.

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati's legacy closely traces the birth and evolution of Silicon Valley.

For nearly six decades, the firm has represented the technology pioneers associated with virtually every milestone innovation.

Today, Wilson Sonsini is synonymous with ushering promising, innovative companies through their business life cycle.

And as our clients have grown, so has our firm. We now represent many of the largest companies in the world-and thousands of the smallest ones, too.

value.Title: Is the best climate bet a cleaner grid or a cleaner sky?

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-5BR3-RXK9-G1DV-00000-00Is the best climate bet a cleaner grid or a cleaner sky?2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisIs the best climate bet a cleaner grid or a cleaner sky?

Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Sarah DeWeerdt

May 12th, 2026 ( Anthropocene Magazine - Delivered by Newstex )

Dollar for dollar, investing in renewable energy provides greater benefits to society than technology to remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, according to a new analysis.

Most previous studies of direct air capture (DAC) have looked at whether it removes more carbon dioxide than it produces, or whether it costs society less to remove a ton of carbon from the atmosphere than it does to leave it there-in effect comparing carbon capture with doing nothing.

'Many analyses ask 'is direct air capture net-negative?' and leave it there, without acknowledging that there is an opportunity cost to investing in direct air capture,' says study team member Yannai Kashtan, a researcher at PSE Healthy Energy, an Oakland, CA-based independent research institute.

Instead, Kashtan and his colleagues set a higher bar for DAC, comparing its return on investment to that of other climate-friendly technologies, namely renewable energy development.

'I was surprised how much the answer [to] 'is DAC worth it?' changes when you change your metric,' Kashtan says.

The researchers modeled the health and climate benefits of investing $100 million in direct air capture versus investing the same amount in utility-scale solar or onshore wind in 22 regions across the United States through 2050.

The public health impact of DAC is often overlooked in studies of the technology. But if the electricity to power DAC comes even partially from a fossil-based grid, it results in sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide, and small particulate matter pollution-while renewables do not.

The researchers modeled four scenarios for the development of DAC technology and performance, analyzing each of these in the context of eight different hypothetical future grid scenarios developed by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Recommended Reading:

New study compares growing corn for energy to solar production. It's no contest.

The results were stark. 'Solar and wind beat direct air capture now and all the way through 2050, even if direct air capture gets substantially cheaper and more energy-efficient,' says Kashtan.

If today's performance of DAC holds-the technology currently requires about 5,500 kilowatt hours of electricity and costs $1,000 to remove one ton of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere-it would have a net negative impact on society through 2050 due to greenhouse gas emissions and harmful air pollution, the researchers found.

Even if DAC energy use falls by more than two-thirds to 1,500 kilowatt hours and its cost by half to $500 per ton of carbon dioxide removed, the benefits of renewables are several-fold greater than those of DAC.

Only in the most optimistic scenario for DAC development-in which these figures fall to 800 kilowatt hours and $100 per ton of carbon dioxide removed-does the technology edge out renewables nationwide. Even then, solar and wind remain the better investment in some regions, such as across the Midwest.

'To be clear, direct air capture can do something solar and wind cannot: reduce atmospheric CO2 concentrations, undoing past damage,' Kashtan says. But until carbon emissions are virtually zeroed out, DAC is highly unlikely to be cost-effective compared to investing in renewables. Kashtan compares the situation to a common-sense principle: 'fix your broken faucet before you start mopping the floor.'

A future analysis could try to find the 'tipping point' where the grid is sufficiently clean that DAC offers greater bang for the buck, says Kashtan.

Source: Kashtan Y.et al.' Direct air capture has substantial health and climate opportunity costs.'Communications Sustainability2026.

Image: Anthropocene Magazine.

value.Title: Environmental Activists Announce New Actions to Fight Sable Pipeline Restart

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-DJF3-RVFR-C22W-00000-00Environmental Activists Announce New Actions to Fight Sable Pipeline Restart2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:29ZLexisNexisLOCAL NEWSEnvironmental Activists Announce New Actions to Fight Sable Pipeline RestartRebecca CarawayNOOZHAWK STAFF WRITER

In the latest attempt to stop Sable Offshore Corp. oil operations off the Santa Barbara County coast, local groups announced Thursday they plan to file a motion to intervene in Sable's production and join the State of California in fighting President Donald Trump's order to reactivate the pipeline.

Spokespeople for the groups the Environmental Defense Center, the Center for Biological Diversity and Earth Justice announced their intentions at Shoreline Park in Santa Barbara alongside local elected officials and a Chumash leader.

"Every day that Sable pumps oil off of our coast increases the risk of another massive oil spill," said Linda Krop, chief counsel for the Environmental Defense Center.

Sable is a Texas-based oil company formed to take over the Santa Ynez Unit of Santa Barbara County platforms, processing and pipeline facilities. An injunction was placed on the pipeline system in the aftermath of the 2015 Refugio oil spill as part of a settlement with the pipeline's previous owners.

The Department of Energy said in March that the pipeline could be allowed to restart under the Defense Production Act.

The Trump administration issued orders to reactivate the pipelines on March 13 "to address supply disruption risks caused by California policies," according to an announcement from the U.S. Department of Energy.

Last month, Santa Barbara County Superior Court Judge Donna Geck ruled that Sable is still required to comply with an injunction barring the restart of its oil and gas production facilities, and that the Trump administration's orders to begin pumping oil do not override the injunction.

Local elected officials, including Rep. Salud Carbajal, D-Santa Barbara, U.S. Sen. Adam Schiff and State Assemblyman Gregg Hart heavily criticized Sable and the Trump administration on Thursday.

Carbajal said the fight against Sable can feel personal, as many remember the impact the 2015 Refugio oil spill had on the local environment.

"Trump and his cronies are telling people that Sable is a domestic solution to the energy crisis, an energy crisis that he created with his idiotic and illegal war with Iran," Carbajal said. "But the reality is that Sable won't produce anywhere near the amount of oil to lower the price families are paying at the pump."

Schiff spoke about the importance of moving away from relying on fossil fuels. He said the community should focus on renewable energy sources and highlight the amount of money oil companies are making.

"(Oil companies are) making record profits. They don't need to be charging the prices we're paying at the pump," Schiff said, adding that he saw "our highest exports of fuel ever from the United States" last week.

"So much of the fuel that is produced here, we're sending out of the country. The only beneficiaries here are the oil industry."

He also spoke out against the Trump administration invoking the Defense Production Act to restart the pipeline.

Mati Waiya, chairman of the Coastal Band of the Chumash Nation, said it was important for everyone to come together, no matter their background, to protect the environment.

"What a day to come together, to really look through the eyes of our ancestors, to listen to the waves and the voices of them that are warning us about the disaster that could happen with Sable restart," Waiya said. "A defense act of war that should have never started in the first place, and we're paying the price."

There are multiple lawsuits in progress over the Sable restart. Krop said the state has a hearing scheduled for June 1 to request a preliminary injunction against Trump's executive order.

The Environmental Defense Center and Sable are scheduled to be back in court in Santa Barbara on May 22 over the alleged violation of the injunction preventing the pipeline restart.

The post Environmental Activists Announce New Actions to Fight Sable Pipeline Restart appeared first on Noozhawk.

value.Title: Battery Energy Storage Systems Rep. Panetta introduces new safety bill

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-CFT3-S0SW-R4RW-00000-00Battery Energy Storage Systems2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisFEATURESPg. 1Battery Energy Storage SystemsRep. Panetta introduces new safety billPK Hattis ; pkhattis @santacruzsentinel.com

WATSONVILLE Rep. Jimmy Panetta, D-Santa Cruz, has authored and introduced a bill with bipartisan support that seeks to allocate federal resources behind greater battery energy storage safety protocols.

According to a release shared Friday by Panetta ’s office, the legislation, co-led by Rep. Pat Harrigan, R-North Carolina, seeks to bolster general safety for the green energy technology by, in part, directing the U.S. Department of Energy to conduct robust energy storage safety research, development and demonstration activities within its energy storage programs.

In addition to existing federal work that studies battery system failures and its causes, Panetta’s bill would target early detection methods and preventative techniques to reduce risks of fires or explosions, such as what occurred at Moss Landing in January 2025.

“As we continue to move towards renewable energy, we must ensure that the corresponding and critical technology is safe and secure,” Panetta said in the release. “Although state-level public utility commissions have the authority and are responsible for ensuring safe and reliable energy service, my bipartisan bill would allow the federal government to make necessary improvements for the research, development, and testing of critical battery storage and clean energy systems. As we strive to reduce our carbon output with clean energy facilities, including battery storage, I believe the federal government has a role to play to ensure the safety and protection of our environment and communities.”

Other provisions of the proposed federal legislation include authorizing $30 million per year for energy storage safety activities in fiscal years 2027 through 2031, and improved coordination between the Department of Energy, the

National Institute of Standards and Technology and the U.S. Fire Administration to develop better testing and validation standards for grid-connected energy storage systems and their associated safety equipment. The bill would also amend federal program direction and strategic planning to incorporate energy storage safety, and seeks to expand energy storage demonstration programs to include two additional demonstrations that would enhance the safety of both short- and long-duration energy storage systems.

“Energy storage is a critical piece of America’s energy future, and we have to make sure we are building it on a foundation that is safe and reliable. These systems are going into homes, onto the grid, and into communities across the country, and right now our testing and safety standards have not kept pace with how fast the technology is moving,” said Harrigan. “This bill closes that gap, puts the right agencies to work on the hard problems, and makes sure we are stress-testing these systems before failures happen in the field, not after.”

According to the release, Panetta and Harrigan’s proposal is supported by American Clean Power Association and the U.S. Energy Storage Coalition. It has also been endorsed by California State Sen. John Laird, who authored Senate Bill 283, which seeks to increase battery storage project safety through increased collaboration with fire officials during the development process. The bill was signed into law last year.

“The Moss Landing fire underscored the urgent need to strengthen safety standards for battery energy storage systems nationwide,” remarked Laird. “California has taken important steps at the state level, but federal partnership is essential to advancing research, improving coordination with fire officials, and developing stronger testing and safety standards. I appreciate Congressman Panetta’s leadership in moving this conversation forward and helping ensure the clean energy transition continues in a way that is both ambitious and safe.”

The bill may draw inspiration from the fire in Moss Landing, but it also emerges as Santa Cruz County residents and political leaders continue to grapple with how to regulate new developments. A 160-unit, 16-acre battery energy storage project from developer New Leaf Energy was proposed in 2024 on Minto Road in unincorporated territory near Watsonville.

The project has elicited intense local scrutiny, especially after the Moss Landing incident. Some residents have raised safety concerns at several community meetings and public forums, while the project’s developer has attempted to alleviate those concerns by pointing out key differences between the proposed project and the Moss Landing site, such as newer battery chemistries and updated storage standards.

In January, the county Board of Supervisors approved a draft ordinance that would establish local rules for these battery projects, but its process took too long and New Leaf Energy announced in early May that it would seek project permits from the California Energy Commission instead.

“Grateful to Congressman Panetta for his leadership on strengthening safety standards for battery storage systems. Since the fires at Moss Landing, I’ve been clear: our communities deserve transparency, accountability, and strong protections,” said Assemblymember Dawn Addis. “The Better Energy Storage and Safety (BESS) Act is an important step to ensure the federal government fully understands the impacts of energy storage in communities like ours so an incident like Moss Landing never happens again.”

value.Title: California Energy Commission Approves Soda Mountain Solar and Battery Storage Project in San Bernardino County

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-NHP3-S9VN-M2PV-00000-00California Energy Commission Approves Soda Mountain Solar and Battery Storage Project in San Bernardino County2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisCalifornia Energy Commission Approves Soda Mountain Solar and Battery Storage Project in San Bernardino County

The California Energy Commission (CEC) last week approved the Soda Mountain Solar Project through its Opt-In Certification program, created to help speed up clean energy permitting and deployment across the state.

Soda Mountain Solar, LLC, a subsidiary of VC Renewables, will construct and operate a combined solar photovoltaic (PV) and battery energy storage system (BESS). The project will be located on approximately 2,670 acres of land administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) California Desert District in San Bernardino County, contingent on BLM approval.

Once built, the nearly $700 million project will generate up to 300 megawatts (MW) of renewable energy from a utility-scale solar PV array and include up to 300 MW of battery storage capable of storing 1,200 megawatt-hours (MWh) of energy. This will strengthen statewide grid reliability and help California move closer to achieving its clean energy and climate goals. The project is expected to create approximately 200 construction jobs and up to 40 part-time operations jobs and additional jobs as needed to conduct routine inspections, maintenance, and security along with millions of dollars in local economic activity and tax revenue to support public services in San Bernardino County.

The project also includes a community benefits agreement that commits the developer to pay $50,000 to Friends of El Mirage, a local nonprofit that supports outdoor recreation and public land stewardship in San Bernardino County. The funding will pay for improvements to amenities at the Rasor Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area.

This project doesnt solely deliver clean, reliable energy it brings jobs and meaningful economic investment to the region, said CEC Commissioner Noemi Gallardo. We are committed to seeing that the benefits of Californias energy transition are felt locally, in the communities where these projects are built.

The Opt-In Certification program is designed to accelerate the development of critical clean energy infrastructure without compromising Californias rigorous environmental protections or commitment to meaningful community engagement, said CEC Chair David Hochschild. Todays approval shows we can move projects forward with urgency and efficiency while preserving the values that matter most to Californians.

Soda Mountain is the second renewable energy project approved through the Opt-In Certification Program. Authorized under Assembly Bill 205, the Opt-In Certification Program provides a consolidated state permitting option for eligible clean energy projects, supporting Californias transition to 100 percent zero-carbon retail electric sales by 2045, as required by Senate Bill 100.

value.Title: 400MWh Weld Energy Storage Project in Platte River , Colorado Advances as PRPA Finishes Key Steps

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJM-35H3-S02V-B1P3-00000-00400MWh Weld Energy Storage Project in Platte River, Colorado Advances as PRPA Finishes Key Steps2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexis400MWh Weld Energy Storage Project in Platte River, Colorado Advances as PRPA Finishes Key Steps

Platte River Power Authority (PRPA) is kicking off construction of the 400MWh Weld Energy Storage project in Colorado. The project is the first for the wholesale power provider for Fort Collins and three other communities. Furthermore, the project will be the utilitys first utility-scale battery project. It is part of its efforts to go as far as it believes it can to rely on all-non carbon energy sources.

Construction is set to commence in May and a groundbreaking ceremony set to happen on June 4. This is according to information presented during the April 30 board meeting. Once complete, the system will be able to store 400 megawatt hours of power.

Moreover, it will allow PRPA to charge the battery when renewable energy is abundant and prices are low and use it later. NextEra Energy Resources will build and own the project, which will be located at the Severance substation, right next to the Black Hollow Sun solar project. Solar and battery storage projects are becoming fundamental with states making more investments to advance renewable energy.

For instance, The Eland Solar-plus-Storage Center in Los Angeles, California developed by Arevon became operational 10 months ago. With 758MW of solar photovoltaic capacity and a 1,200MWh battery energy storage system, the $2 billion mega-project represents a major leap toward LAs clean energy goals.

Other Energy Projects by PRPA other than Weld Energy Storage Project in Colorado

Other than the Weld Energy Storage project in Colorado, PRPA is focused on construction of five aeroderivative gas turbines. The utility says the turbines are needed to provide dispatchable capacity that can be fired up when renewable sources like wind and solar are not meeting the demand. However, the move has drawn criticism from environmental advocates.

They note that the $623 million investment could become a stranded asset that ratepayers will have to fund even if they are cleaner alternatives available after it is built. However, PRPA notes the technology is actually what enables it to pursue renewables more aggressively. It notes it is because it provides a way to fill the gaps when renewables are not meeting demand. The state construction permit, another step in the process to complete the project, was issued April 15.

The state utility has already been granted a 1041 permit from Larimer County. The turbines are expected to be up and running before the scheduled closure of the Rawhide 1 coal unit in December 2029. However, there will be a 180-day period where the coal plant will be allowed to operate at the same time as the new turbines while they are being tested. Once the turbines are cleared for commercial dispatch, Rawhide 1 will close.

Project Factsheet

Project Name: Weld Energy Storage Project

Location: Platte River region, Colorado, USA (Severance Substation)

Estimated Investment Value: Not disclosed

Project Type: Utility-scale battery energy storage system

Timeline

April 2026: Key permits secured and approvals completed

May 2026: Construction commencement

June 4, 2026: Groundbreaking ceremony scheduled

Completion: Not officially disclosed

Site & Scale

Storage Capacity: 400 MWh

Project Type: Battery energy storage system (BESS)

Location Detail: Adjacent to Black Hollow solar project

Primary Use: Store excess renewable energy and dispatch when needed

Project Teams

Utility / Offtaker: Platte River Power Authority (PRPA)

Developer / Owner / Builder: NextEra Energy Resources

Infrastructure Scope

400MWh battery storage installation

Integration with nearby solar generation (Black Hollow Solar Project)

Connection to Severance substation

Grid support systems for load balancing and dispatch

Strategic Objectives

Support transition to non-carbon energy sources

Improve grid reliability and flexibility

Store excess renewable energy during low demand periods

Reduce reliance on fossil fuel peaking generation

Enable higher penetration of wind and solar energy

Challenges

Intermittency of renewable energy sources

Dependence on complementary systems like gas turbines

Criticism over parallel investment in gas infrastructure

Uncertain long-term cost dynamics of storage vs alternatives

Current Status

State construction permit issued (April 15, 2026)

1041 permit granted by Larimer County

Construction set to begin with groundbreaking in June

Marks PRPAs first utility-scale battery storage project

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website
