value.Title: Billionaires want to live forever

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-7TF3-RS1T-919C-00000-00Billionaires want to live forever2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:28ZLexisNexisLOCAL NEWSBillionaires want to live forever

COMMENTARY

Too frequently, in establishment press such as the New York Times, there is yet another story about some billionaire or other funding longevity research. Or injecting himself with his child’s blood plasma, eating a professionally curated diet once or twice a day, shooting themselves up with some sort of gene therapy, taking insane amounts of supplements, and all the other "me-centered" obsessions. Apparently, there are longevity clinics, that Yahoo news says are "targeted toward higher income households."

And there are lots of real and fake medical practitioners who are taking advantage of these men (and it is almost all men) like a CEO, Jan Gerber, quoted in a NY Post story "I’ve got the funds, but I’ve limited years." This is big business!

New York Times, April 29, 2026: "Fueling this industry are some of the wealthiest men in the world, who are trying to finance their way to longer life, either by biohacking their own health or pouring money into the research. Major figures in Silicon Valley, including Peter Thiel, Larry Ellison and Sam Altman, have collectively invested billions in biotech companies and research centers devoted to slowing the aging process."

Notice anything here? These are the same men who are systematically demolishing our collective environment with their water hogging, energy grubbing data centers that sit on valuable land that could be left to nature, farmed, or used to build needed housing. These are the same men who fund Donald Trump and the sensationally hypocritical Mr. MAHA, RFK Jr. These are the same men who reject all regulation of pesticides, industrial emissions, exposure to toxins that kill working stiffs, funding for upgrading municipal water and energy systems to protect communities.

These guys scoff at all renewable energy that might save us from the worse effects of climate catastrophe. The very same men who pollute our collective environment with their private jets, mega-yachts, massive and multiple dwellings, and all the other excesses of this greedy class.

The statistics are daunting.

According to Oxfam, billionaires emit more carbon in 90 minutes than the average person uses in a lifetime.

This extreme disparity the idea that the super rich believe they will extend their lifespans to 200 or longer, while they are heavily contributing to the deaths of billions of their fellow humans is the true horror of this moment in history. Diseases of the industrial age such as cancer and Parkinson’s are directly related to the emissions the industries of the super-rich have spent bigly to deregulate. And these diseases are flourishing like never before.

When there is a heat dome caused directly by the excess carbon and other greenhouse gasses, the super-rich may be high up at their retreats in Switzerland or the Rockies while people in India are dying from exposure. Or when a huge storm is predicted, people in island nations or coastal areas are forced to leave their only home, or even drown, these guys are nowhere to be seen.

While we clearly will never be able to instill human decency, a conscience, or even a slight twinge of caring about the humans who they consider their inferiors, our first job is to work to defeat the political protectors of these climate criminals. As long as the Republicans and corporate Democrats prevail, we will not begin to control the excesses of this "let them eat cake" moment. At the current time, we need to work with our states to regulate the unlimited uses of agricultural and industrial toxins, as well as support renewables and limit greenhouse gas emissions. For example, my state, Vermont, has pending legislation to ban one of the worst pesticides, paraquat, known to cause Parkinson’s and cancer as do about 10 other states because we know Trump’s government is hopelessly corrupt. We can work with our states to force these elites to pay their fair share of taxes. And in the longer run, we will all need to come together to defeat the Republican authoritarian regime.

Personally, the most exciting moments in my life have been from working with others to improve the world, by opposing war, closing the nearby and badly aging nuclear reactor, or creating Everyone’s Books, which I believe continues to enrich my community. Some-how, the idea of living forever as a person whose sole focus is yourself and your wealth is pretty unappealing, boys.

Nancy Braus is a long-time political activist who writes from Guilford. The opinions expressed by columnists do not necessarily reflect the views of Vermont News & Media.

Nancy Braus Notes from a Vermont Activist

DASHBOARD: ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.

PermID4296560718Websitehttp://www.ecotricity.co.ukIndustryEnergy & Renewables

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
